payload.prompt:
Okay, I don't think it actually does save progress, so let's remove the save progress button and just have an export. Unless we have a save that actually works. The goal is for this to come back, and as we have these meetings, we can delegate tasks to Matthew appropriately based on what he gives high confidence intervals for. The sliding scale is a little, probably too much on the tens, so let's just go with a five-point scale, which is low confidence to high confidence. And where he can see each point on the five-point scale and just click it to decide 

Also, I think there are way too many things, so can we think about what tasks are basically very similar and consolidate them into a much shorter list form? Let me know if that's a reasonable request. Or if you think we lose something meaningful in doing this

tool_input.body: Client task confidence scoring for transition planning
tool_input.htmlBody:
Client Task Confidence Scoring
CEO • BronxWorks • HELP USA

Hey Matthew,

Following up on our conversation about transitioning CEO, BronxWorks, and HELP USA — I had Vishali go through all of our Fathom transcripts, emails, and Asana tasks for the last six months and put together a comprehensive list of everything I do for each of these three clients.

The result is an interactive scoring tool where you can rate your confidence level (1–5) on each task. This will help us figure out what you can jump right into, what needs some ramp-up time, and what I should keep handling for now.

Scoring Tool
matthew-capacity-scoring.netlify.app
Password: Matthew-2026!

How it works:

1
 Click each client to expand their task list. Each has a meeting schedule at the top so you can see the time commitments.
2
 Rate each task 1–5 based on your confidence. Mark N/A for anything that wouldn't transfer to you.
3
 Use the notes field under each client for any questions or thoughts specific to that engagement.
4
 When you're done, click Export Results — it downloads a text file. Send that file back to me so I can review your scores and we can plan the handoff.
Confidence Scale
1 Need significant guidance    2 Could learn with support    3 Moderate confidence
4 Comfortable, minimal help    5 I've got this

No rush, but whenever you can get to it would be great — it'll help me start planning the introductions and transition timeline. Let me know if you have any questions.

Thanks, bro.

Joshua

Meet the Moment • mtm.now
This email was drafted with AI assistance.

payload.last_assistant_message:
It's not auto-run currently — it's intentionally just a printed reminder. But here's the thing: I think auto-running it after every `/upgrade` poll would actually be the wrong move.

The distinction is:

- **`/upgrade` (poll)** = "What's new out there?" — reads external sources, no code changes. The checklist doesn't apply because nothing changed.
- **Implementing an improvement** = actual code/config changes. *This* is when the checklist matters.

Running hygiene + inventory + pentest after every poll would add 2-3 minutes of noise with zero value, since we didn't change anything.

**What would make sense:** auto-run the checklist only when an improvement is actually *implemented* during the session — i.e., after a status change to `completed`. That way it triggers exactly when it's useful.

Want me to wire that up as a hook, or keep it as a printed reminder that I just execute when relevant? The hook approach is cleaner but adds complexity; the reminder approach works fine given that I'm present for every implementation anyway.
